questions.question: What is 'Level 1: Internal Listening' focused on?
questions.explanation: Internal listening is the enemy of discovery. You are so busy worrying about your next line that you ignore the reality of the conversation.

questions.question: What is 'Mirroring'?

options.text: Repeating the last 1-3 words they said as a question to encourage elaboration

questions.explanation: Mirroring is a verbal technique (from Chris Voss) that prompts the other person to explain further without you having to formulate a new question.

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Labeling'?

options.text: To name their emotion so they feel heard and understood

questions.explanation: 'It sounds like you are frustrated.' This label validates their feeling. Once a person feels heard, their resistance drops.

questions.question: Why should you Summarize every 5-10 minutes?

options.text: To ensure alignment and allow them to correct any misunderstandings

questions.explanation: Summarizing creates 'Checkpoints.' If you misunderstood something at minute 5, you don't want to find that out at minute 30. Correct it early.

questions.question: When you are silent, the prospect will often:

options.text: Fill the silence with more truth (the real answer)

questions.explanation: Silence creates social pressure. To relieve that pressure, the prospect keeps talking, often revealing the 'Phase 2' truth they held back initially.
